description:
You are a container-platform specialist preparing a new micro-service host.  
Your goal is to set up an SSH keypair that other automation will consume and to leave an auditable trace of what you did.

Expected final state that the automated grader WILL check:

1. Keypair  
   a. Private key: /home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa  
   b. Public  key: /home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa.pub  

   Both files must exist.  
   • The private key must be in OpenSSH PEM format – the very first line of the file has to be “-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----”.  
   • The public key must be exactly one single-line entry that starts with the literal text “ssh-rsa ” (note the space) and ends with the default comment OpenSSH places after the key (typically “microservice_rsa” or “microservice_rsa@<hostname>”).  
   • The key size must be 4096 bits and the passphrase must be empty.

2. Authorized-keys hand-off  
   • Create the directory /home/user/micro_keys  
   • Create /home/user/micro_keys/authorized_keys_micro containing exactly the same one-line public key that is in /home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa.pub (verbatim copy, no extra blank lines).  

3. Audit log  
   Produce a plaintext log file /home/user/ssh_setup.log with exactly the four lines below, each on its own line and in this exact order:

KEYPAIR_CREATED=/home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa  
PUBKEY_APPENDED=/home/user/micro_keys/authorized_keys_micro  
KEYTYPE=RSA4096  
PASSPHRASE=none  

The file must end with a trailing newline (so the grader’s wc -l count is 4).

What you need to do (do NOT list the commands here, just perform them in the terminal):

• Generate a new 4096-bit RSA keypair with an empty passphrase and the precise filenames given above.  
• Make sure the .ssh directory exists and has at least u+r permissions so the grader can read the files.  
• Copy/append the public key into /home/user/micro_keys/authorized_keys_micro. Create the directory beforehand if it does not exist.  
• Create the log file with the four required lines.

No other files should be altered or created. The grader will fail the task if:

• Either key file is missing or empty.  
• The public key line is wrapped or followed by extra lines.  
• The audit log deviates in spelling, order, or number of lines.

Once done, you are finished.
truth:
Final system state for grading:

Files that must exist:

1. /home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa  
   • ASCII text, begins with “-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----”.  
   • Must contain at least 3 lines and end with “-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----\n”.

2. /home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa.pub  
   • Single-line file that matches regex: ^ssh\-rsa [A-Za-z0-9+/=]+ .*$\n  
   • Content must be identical to the single line inside /home/user/micro_keys/authorized_keys_micro.

3. /home/user/micro_keys/authorized_keys_micro  
   • Single-line file, identical to /home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa.pub.

4. /home/user/ssh_setup.log  
   • Exactly these bytes (including final newline):  
     KEYPAIR_CREATED=/home/user/.ssh/microservice_rsa\n  
     PUBKEY_APPENDED=/home/user/micro_keys/authorized_keys_micro\n  
     KEYTYPE=RSA4096\n  
     PASSPHRASE=none\n  

Permissions (not strictly required for pass): files must be readable by the current user.

No other files or directories should be touched.
